title: Macro Briefing — 2026-06-29
subtitle: Generated 09:56 AM ET on Monday, June 29, 2026
markdown:
# Macro Briefing — 2026-06-29
_Generated 09:56 AM ET on Monday, June 29, 2026_

## Market Summary
_LLM-augmented: false_

The Fed is in HAWKISH_TIGHTENING — front-end anchored, long-end driven by term premium and supply. With RESTRICTIVE_HOLD as the most probable next state (17%), the curve should remain flat to inverted while rates stay restrictive. 10y yield at 4.40 % yield; 2s10s at +31 bp. Front-end is anchored by Fed guidance; back-end takes its cue from supply, term premium and growth-data risk.

Curve bias: flattening pressure persists while the Fed holds. Fade 2s10s above +45 bp; receive 5s on a 50 bp dip.

GBP/USD: USD relatively tighter (HAWKISH_TIGHTENING) vs GBP (RESTRICTIVE_HOLD) — supports USD strength in this pair.

EUR/USD: neutral bias (EUR is in HAWKISH_TIGHTENING (32% confidence), USD is in HAWKISH_TIGHTENING (84% confidence). Regime divergence score: +0 → ESTR/SOFR should be neutral.) GBP/USD: neutral bias (GBP is in RESTRICTIVE_HOLD (57% confidence), USD is in HAWKISH_TIGHTENING (84% confidence). Regime divergence score: +0 → SONIA/SOFR should be neutral.)

**Key levels (FRED):**
- Fed Target Range Lower: 3.50 % annualised (as of 2026-06-29)
- Fed Target Range Upper: 3.75 % annualised (as of 2026-06-29)
- Fed Funds Effective Rate: 3.63 % annualised (as of 2026-05-01)
- US CPI YoY: 4.17 % YoY (as of 2026-05-01)
- US Core CPI YoY: 2.82 % YoY (as of 2026-05-01)
- US PCE YoY: 4.07 % YoY (as of 2026-05-01)
- US Core PCE YoY: 3.41 % YoY (as of 2026-05-01)
- US Unemployment Rate: 4.30% (as of 2026-05-01)
- US 10Y Treasury Yield: 4.40 % yield (as of 2026-06-25)
- US 2Y Treasury Yield: 4.09 % yield (as of 2026-06-25)
- US 30Y Treasury Yield: 4.86 % yield (as of 2026-06-25)
- 10Y-2Y Spread: +31 bp (as of 2026-06-26)
- SOFR: 3.62 % annualised (as of 2026-06-26)
- ON RRP Take-up: $6.43bn (as of 2026-06-26)
- IORB: 3.65 % annualised (as of 2026-06-29)

## Central Bank Watch

**Fed:**

- Next meeting: [pending cb_calendar.yaml verification]
- _Analyst view:_ Restrictive hold with dovish drift; first cut delivered if labour softens through Aug NFP.
The Fed is actively tightening. Policy rate trajectory is upward — front-end bears, curve flattens. Terminal rate is approaching — any softening in guidance signals the pivot is near.
**Next move: cut/dovish** (17% probability). Hold probability: 75%. Terminal rate bias: lower than market.
Base case (75%): HAWKISH_TIGHTENING holds. Tail risk (17%): RESTRICTIVE_HOLD.
- Fed Funds Effective Rate: 3.63 % annualised (as of 2026-05-01)
- SOFR: 3.62 % annualised (as of 2026-06-26)
- Fed Target Range Upper: 3.75 % annualised (as of 2026-06-29)

**ECB:**

- Last meeting: [pending cb_calendar.yaml verification]
- Next meeting: [pending cb_calendar.yaml verification]
- _Analyst view:_ End-of-cycle hold; balance of risks favours patience while wages cool.
The ECB is actively tightening. Policy rate trajectory is upward — front-end bears, curve flattens. Terminal rate is approaching — any softening in guidance signals the pivot is near.
**Next move: cut/dovish** (20% probability). Hold probability: 71%. Terminal rate bias: lower than market.
Base case (71%): HAWKISH_TIGHTENING holds. Tail risk (20%): RESTRICTIVE_HOLD.

**BOE:**

- Last meeting: [pending cb_calendar.yaml verification]
- Next meeting: [pending cb_calendar.yaml verification]
- _Analyst view:_ Gradual easing with services CPI sticky; cuts come in measured 25 bp steps.
The BOE is holding at restrictive levels. Front-end is anchored by the hold, but the market is forward-looking — OIS is already pricing the first cut. Curve flattening pressure persists until pivot signals emerge.
**Next move: cut/dovish** (18% probability). Hold probability: 72%. Terminal rate bias: lower than market.
Base case (72%): RESTRICTIVE_HOLD holds. Tail risk (18%): TRANSITION_EASING.

**BOJ:**
- Policy rate: [UNAVAILABLE — requires BOJ stats endpoint]
- Last meeting: [pending cb_calendar.yaml verification]
- Next meeting: [pending cb_calendar.yaml verification]
- _Analyst view:_ Cautious normalisation; FX weakness vs USD is the political/inflation pressure point.
2y rate diff vs USD at z = +0.70 — market positioning is dovish relative to USD.

**Balance Sheet / QT:** No fresh QT headlines today. Treasury runoff cap remains at $25bn/month per the Mar-25 slowdown; ECB APP redemptions ~€20bn/mo, PEPP fully reinvested through Dec-25.

**Relative Hawkishness Ranking (per-CB 2y rate-diff z vs USD baseline):**
1. **BOE** — terminal bias: modestly hawkish vs USD (z=-0.86)
2. **Fed** — next move: cut/dovish; terminal bias: lower than market (rate-z baseline)
3. **BOJ** — terminal bias: modestly dovish vs USD (z=+0.70)
4. **ECB** — terminal bias: dovish vs USD (z=+1.47)

**Pairwise RV (USD rate differentials, z-score vs trailing 60obs):**
- USD-EUR: 2y diff z = +1.47 (USD-dovish stretched). RV expression: pay 2y EUR vs receive 2y USD if z drifts back to zero.
- USD-GBP: 2y diff z = -0.86 (in-range). RV expression: receive 2y GBP vs pay 2y USD on extreme negative z; reverse on positive.
- USD-JPY: 2y diff z = +0.70 (in-range). RV expression: 2y USD-JPY rate diff carries USD/JPY spot — fade extreme z.

## Rates Market Assessment

**USD**: Currently in HAWKISH_TIGHTENING (84% confidence). Next move: **cut/dovish** (17% probability). Hold probability: 75%. Base case (75%): HAWKISH_TIGHTENING holds. Tail risk (17%): RESTRICTIVE_HOLD. Terminal rate bias: lower than market.

### Yield Curve & Term Premium
_LLM-augmented: false_
With the Fed in HAWKISH_TIGHTENING, the curve is under flattening pressure. Front-end is anchored by restrictive rates; long-end is driven by term premium and supply. Fade 2s10s above +45 bp; receive 5s on a 50 bp dip.
- US 2Y Treasury Yield: 4.09% (1d -2 bp, vs 2026-06-25)
- US 10Y Treasury Yield: 4.40% (1d -1 bp, vs 2026-06-25)
- US 30Y Treasury Yield: 4.86% (1d +0 bp, vs 2026-06-25)
- 2s10s Spread: +31 bp (1d +0 bp, vs 2026-06-26)
- US Real 10Y Yield (TIPS): 2.19 % yield (as of 2026-06-25)
- US 5Y5Y Forward Breakeven: 2.19% (as of 2026-06-26)

### SOFR Futures & Money Markets
With the Fed on hold/tightening, SOFR front-end is anchored near the target rate. Expect PCA dislocations in Reds/Greens/Blues for relative value — calendar spreads and flies are the cleanest expressions.
- Front-end OIS-implied path (FRED proxy, pending CME SR3 wiring): 1M 3.70 % yield | 3M 3.84 % yield | 6M 3.95 % yield | 1Y 3.96 % yield
- ON RRP take-up: $6.43bn (as of 2026-06-26)
- SOFR-IORB spread: -3.0 bp (SOFR 3.62% vs IORB 3.65%)
- Fed Funds Effective Rate: 3.63 % annualised (as of 2026-05-01)
- SOFR: 3.62 % annualised (as of 2026-06-26)

### Volatility Surface
- Realised 10y UST yield vol (20d ann): 71 bp/day. Without a live swaption feed we cannot quote 3m1y implied here; vol-of-vol watch keys off this realised number day-to-day.

### Swap Spreads & Funding
- _Spread levels pending DTCC SDR public-swap fetch (round-2 data item). Anchor view from 2024-26 distribution: 10y SS deeply negative (≈ -50 bp) on SLR-constrained dealer balance sheets and heavy coupon supply._

### FX Volatility & Hedging Flows

### FX Carry & Forward Dynamics
- **EUR/USD**: Both in HAWKISH_TIGHTENING — divergence-driven carry is dormant; trade growth-differential and positioning instead.
- **GBP/USD**: USD in HAWKISH_TIGHTENING vs GBP in RESTRICTIVE_HOLD — rate differential favors USD. Positive USD carry in this pair. Basis implication: widening pressure as USD funding premium rises.

- USD/JPY: carry-to-vol = +0.24 (z=+0.70, RV30d=3.0%) — long-carry tilt
- AUD/USD: carry-to-vol = +0.14 (z=+1.11, RV30d=7.8%) — long-carry tilt
- GBP/USD: carry-to-vol = -0.14 (z=-0.86, RV30d=6.4%) — short-carry tilt

## Cross-Currency Basis

_Basis levels are 1y anchors pending DTCC SDR public-swap fetch (round-2). Action tag is derived from the live 2y rate-diff z-score against USD._

**ESTR/SOFR:**
- 2y rate diff vs USD z = +1.47.
- Anchor 1y basis: -22 bp _anchor — DTCC fetch pending round-2_
- Driver: ECB APP redemptions + Yankee / reverse-Yankee issuance pipeline (EIB/KfW)
- **bias: widen**

**SONIA/SOFR:**
- 2y rate diff vs USD z = -0.86.
- Anchor 1y basis: -15 bp _anchor — DTCC fetch pending round-2_
- Driver: SONIA-SOFR rate gap + UK clearer USD funding demand at quarter-end
- **bias: tighten**

**TONAR/SOFR:**
- 2y rate diff vs USD z = +0.70.
- Anchor 1y basis: -42 bp _anchor — DTCC fetch pending round-2_
- Driver: Hedged JGB carry + MoF / Japanese lifer USD-asset hedge ratios

**AONIA/SOFR:**
- 2y rate diff vs USD z = +1.11.
- Anchor 1y basis: -7 bp _anchor — DTCC fetch pending round-2_
- Driver: AONIA-SOFR rate gap + Aussie semi-government issuance hedging
- **bias: widen**

**SARON/SOFR:**

- Anchor 1y basis: -28 bp _anchor — DTCC fetch pending round-2_
- Driver: SNB sight-deposit dynamics + reverse-Yankee CHF→USD funding

## Systematic Signal Context

**CB Balance Sheet Expansion/Contraction:**
- _No fresh QT headlines today. Working assumption: Fed at $25bn/mo Treasury runoff cap (Mar-25 slowdown); ECB APP redemptions ~€20bn/mo; BOJ holdings drifting flat as JGB sales pace remains slow._

**SOFR Front-End Slope:**
- SOFR: 3.62 % annualised (as of 2026-06-26)
- _Implication for 2Y basis: steep = easing priced = basis tightener_

**Swaption Vol (realised 10y proxy):**
- Realised 10y UST yield vol (20d ann): 71 bp/day — stand-in until swaption surface is wired.

**Composite Signals by Currency:**
- **EUR**: 2Y signal = neutral basis; 10Y signal = neutral. Regime: HAWKISH_TIGHTENING. EUR is in HAWKISH_TIGHTENING (32% confidence), USD is in HAWKISH_TIGHTENING (84% confidence). Regime divergence score: +0 → ESTR/SOFR should be neutral.
- **GBP**: 2Y signal = neutral basis; 10Y signal = neutral. Regime: RESTRICTIVE_HOLD. GBP is in RESTRICTIVE_HOLD (57% confidence), USD is in HAWKISH_TIGHTENING (84% confidence). Regime divergence score: +0 → SONIA/SOFR should be neutral.
- **JPY**: 2Y signal = neutral basis; 10Y signal = neutral. Regime: Unknown. JPY-USD regime states not yet classified; basis signal driven by flow/seasonals.
- **AUD**: 2Y signal = neutral basis; 10Y signal = neutral. Regime: Unknown. AUD-USD regime states not yet classified; basis signal driven by flow/seasonals.

**SOFR Curve RV:**
- _PCA residuals on the SOFR strip and 2s5s10s / 5s10s30s fly z-scores depend on CME SOFR-futures settles + DTCC SDR rate prints — both are round-2 data items. Use the FRED rate panel above for the directional view._

## Key Events Ahead

**Data Releases:**
- 2026-06-30: EZ Flash CPI (June) [EUR]
- 2026-07-02: US NFP (June) [USD]
- 2026-07-08: FOMC minutes (Jun meeting) [USD]
- 2026-07-15: US CPI (June) [USD]

**From the RSS digest:**
- US CPI YoY: 4.17 % YoY (as of 2026-05-01)
- US Core CPI YoY: 2.82 % YoY (as of 2026-05-01)
- US PCE YoY: 4.07 % YoY (as of 2026-05-01)
- US Core PCE YoY: 3.41 % YoY (as of 2026-05-01)
- US Unemployment Rate: 4.30% (as of 2026-05-01)

## Trade Construction Context

_Each trade lists structure, entry/target/stop, conviction, horizon, carry/roll, sizing, rationale, catalyst, risks, and the explicit invalidating condition._

### Trade 1 — Receive 1y1y USD OIS (proxy: 2xDGS2 - DGS1) vs SR3 white-pack hedge

- **Instrument class:** rates_linear
- **Structure:** Receive 1y1y USD OIS at $10k DV01. Proxy entry computed live as 2*DGS2 - DGS1 = 2*4.09% - 3.96% = 4.22% (linear forward approximation valid at short tenors). Hedge: pay equivalent DV01 of the SR3 white-pack average (SR3 strip levels not in pipeline; size to plan, mark-to-market on entry).
- **Entry:** 1y1y OIS proxy at 4.22% (FRED DGS1 3.96% / DGS2 4.09%, as of 2026-06-25)
- **Target:** 3.97% (-25 bp from entry) as H2-26 cuts get priced more aggressively
- **Stop:** 4.42% (+20 bp from entry) — implies cuts get un-priced
- **Conviction:** MED
- **Horizon:** 6-10 weeks (through Aug FOMC)
- **Carry & roll:** +1.8 bp/m (1y1y rolls down the SOFR curve; live SR3 strip wiring required for exact roll)
- **Sizing:** $10k DV01 per leg, DV01-neutral. Budget $50k DV01.
- **Rationale:** Fed has held since May 2025 and labour is softening (UR drifting to 4.3%). The path Whites imply underestimates the risk that H2 data breaks the stay-on-hold consensus. The 1y1y point captures the FOMC reaction function cleanly without taking individual-meeting timing risk; the SR3 leg isolates the receive-the-cut-cycle convexity.
- **Catalyst:** Jul 24 NFP, Aug 12 CPI, Sep 17 FOMC
- **Risks:**
 - Hot July CPI re-prices the easing cycle out — front-end sells off.
 - Treasury supply spike forces term premium higher, dragging 1y1y up.
 - ON RRP collapses, funding tightness drags SOFR fixings higher.
- **Invalidates the thesis:** Powell explicit statement that the FOMC is biased to hold through year-end.

### Trade 2 — 2s10s UST curve steepener (live FRED-derived)

- **Instrument class:** rates_linear
- **Structure:** Receive 2y / Pay 10y, DV01-neutral. Entry computed live from FRED DGS2 (4.09%) and DGS10 (4.40%) as of 2026-06-25.
- **Entry:** 2s10s at +31 bp
- **Target:** +46 bp (+15 bp on trade)
- **Stop:** +21 bp (-10 bp on trade)
- **Conviction:** MED
- **Horizon:** 6-10 weeks
- **Carry & roll:** Roll-down depends on forwards; rough +0.3 bp/m at current curve shape
- **Sizing:** DV01-matched. $1bn 10y notional ≈ $850k DV01; budget $100k portfolio DV01.
- **Rationale:** 2s10s at +31 bp — in-range. Mild steepener bias on the working assumption that the next regime move is toward easing. Trade is constructed entirely from FRED constant-maturity yields, so all entry/target/stop levels are auditable end-to-end.
- **Catalyst:** Treasury QRA Jul 31; Aug NFP; Sep FOMC
- **Risks:**
 - Term-premium shock (long-end shoots wider) reverses the move.
 - Supply surprise at the August refunding cheapens 10s.
 - Risk-off flight-to-quality bull-flattens the curve.
- **Invalidates the thesis:** Spread breaches the stop in a single session on a known catalyst (NFP / FOMC / refunding).

### FX Volatility & Hedging Flows
- _ATM vol grid + 25-delta risk reversals pending CME FX-options settlement-file integration (round-2). Anchor view: JPY-side risk reversals priced for USD-call demand near BoJ rate-check thresholds._

### FX Carry & Forward Dynamics
- **EUR/USD**: Both in HAWKISH_TIGHTENING — divergence-driven carry is dormant; trade growth-differential and positioning instead.
- **GBP/USD**: USD in HAWKISH_TIGHTENING vs GBP in RESTRICTIVE_HOLD — rate differential favors USD. Positive USD carry in this pair. Basis implication: widening pressure as USD funding premium rises.

sections.title: Cross-Currency Basis
sections.markdown:
_Basis levels are 1y anchors pending DTCC SDR public-swap fetch (round-2). Action tag is derived from the live 2y rate-diff z-score against USD._

**ESTR/SOFR:**
- 2y rate diff vs USD z = +1.47.
- Anchor 1y basis: -22 bp _anchor — DTCC fetch pending round-2_
- Driver: ECB APP redemptions + Yankee / reverse-Yankee issuance pipeline (EIB/KfW)
- **bias: widen**

**SONIA/SOFR:**
- 2y rate diff vs USD z = -0.86.
- Anchor 1y basis: -15 bp _anchor — DTCC fetch pending round-2_
- Driver: SONIA-SOFR rate gap + UK clearer USD funding demand at quarter-end
- **bias: tighten**

**TONAR/SOFR:**
- 2y rate diff vs USD z = +0.70.
- Anchor 1y basis: -42 bp _anchor — DTCC fetch pending round-2_
- Driver: Hedged JGB carry + MoF / Japanese lifer USD-asset hedge ratios

- 2y rate diff vs USD z = +1.11.
- Anchor 1y basis: -7 bp _anchor — DTCC fetch pending round-2_
- Driver: AONIA-SOFR rate gap + Aussie semi-government issuance hedging

**SARON/SOFR:**
- 2y rate diff vs USD z = +0.00.
- Anchor 1y basis: -28 bp _anchor — DTCC fetch pending round-2_
- Driver: SNB sight-deposit dynamics + reverse-Yankee CHF→USD funding

sections.title: Systematic Signal Context
sections.markdown:
**CB Balance Sheet Expansion/Contraction:**
- _No fresh QT headlines today. Working assumption: Fed at $25bn/mo Treasury runoff cap (Mar-25 slowdown); ECB APP redemptions ~€20bn/mo; BOJ holdings drifting flat as JGB sales pace remains slow._

**SOFR Front-End Slope:**
- SOFR: 3.62 % annualised (as of 2026-06-26)
- _Implication for 2Y basis: steep = easing priced = basis tightener_

**Swaption Vol (realised 10y proxy):**
- Realised 10y UST yield vol (20d ann): 71 bp/day — stand-in until swaption surface is wired.

**Composite Signals by Currency:**
- **EUR**: 2Y signal = neutral basis; 10Y signal = neutral. Regime: HAWKISH_TIGHTENING. EUR is in HAWKISH_TIGHTENING (32% confidence), USD is in HAWKISH_TIGHTENING (84% confidence). Regime divergence score: +0 → ESTR/SOFR should be neutral.
- **GBP**: 2Y signal = neutral basis; 10Y signal = neutral. Regime: RESTRICTIVE_HOLD. GBP is in RESTRICTIVE_HOLD (57% confidence), USD is in HAWKISH_TIGHTENING (84% confidence). Regime divergence score: +0 → SONIA/SOFR should be neutral.
- **JPY**: 2Y signal = neutral basis; 10Y signal = neutral. Regime: Unknown. JPY-USD regime states not yet classified; basis signal driven by flow/seasonals.
- **AUD**: 2Y signal = neutral basis; 10Y signal = neutral. Regime: Unknown. AUD-USD regime states not yet classified; basis signal driven by flow/seasonals.

**SOFR Curve RV:**
- _PCA residuals on the SOFR strip and 2s5s10s / 5s10s30s fly z-scores depend on CME SOFR-futures settles + DTCC SDR rate prints — both are round-2 data items. Use the FRED rate panel above for the directional view._

sections.title: Trade Construction Context
sections.markdown:
_Each trade lists structure, entry/target/stop, conviction, horizon, carry/roll, sizing, rationale, catalyst, risks, and the explicit invalidating condition._

### Trade 1 — Receive 1y1y USD OIS (proxy: 2xDGS2 - DGS1) vs SR3 white-pack hedge

- **Instrument class:** rates_linear
- **Structure:** Receive 1y1y USD OIS at $10k DV01. Proxy entry computed live as 2*DGS2 - DGS1 = 2*4.09% - 3.96% = 4.22% (linear forward approximation valid at short tenors). Hedge: pay equivalent DV01 of the SR3 white-pack average (SR3 strip levels not in pipeline; size to plan, mark-to-market on entry).
- **Entry:** 1y1y OIS proxy at 4.22% (FRED DGS1 3.96% / DGS2 4.09%, as of 2026-06-25)
- **Target:** 3.97% (-25 bp from entry) as H2-26 cuts get priced more aggressively
- **Stop:** 4.42% (+20 bp from entry) — implies cuts get un-priced
- **Conviction:** MED
- **Horizon:** 6-10 weeks (through Aug FOMC)
- **Carry & roll:** +1.8 bp/m (1y1y rolls down the SOFR curve; live SR3 strip wiring required for exact roll)
- **Sizing:** $10k DV01 per leg, DV01-neutral. Budget $50k DV01.
- **Rationale:** Fed has held since May 2025 and labour is softening (UR drifting to 4.3%). The path Whites imply underestimates the risk that H2 data breaks the stay-on-hold consensus. The 1y1y point captures the FOMC reaction function cleanly without taking individual-meeting timing risk; the SR3 leg isolates the receive-the-cut-cycle convexity.
- **Catalyst:** Jul 24 NFP, Aug 12 CPI, Sep 17 FOMC
- **Risks:**
 - Hot July CPI re-prices the easing cycle out — front-end sells off.
 - Treasury supply spike forces term premium higher, dragging 1y1y up.
 - ON RRP collapses, funding tightness drags SOFR fixings higher.
- **Invalidates the thesis:** Powell explicit statement that the FOMC is biased to hold through year-end.

### Trade 2 — 2s10s UST curve steepener (live FRED-derived)

- **Instrument class:** rates_linear
- **Structure:** Receive 2y / Pay 10y, DV01-neutral. Entry computed live from FRED DGS2 (4.09%) and DGS10 (4.40%) as of 2026-06-25.
- **Entry:** 2s10s at +31 bp
- **Target:** +46 bp (+15 bp on trade)
- **Stop:** +21 bp (-10 bp on trade)
- **Conviction:** MED
- **Horizon:** 6-10 weeks
- **Carry & roll:** Roll-down depends on forwards; rough +0.3 bp/m at current curve shape
- **Sizing:** DV01-matched. $1bn 10y notional ≈ $850k DV01; budget $100k portfolio DV01.
- **Rationale:** 2s10s at +31 bp — in-range. Mild steepener bias on the working assumption that the next regime move is toward easing. Trade is constructed entirely from FRED constant-maturity yields, so all entry/target/stop levels are auditable end-to-end.
- **Catalyst:** Treasury QRA Jul 31; Aug NFP; Sep FOMC
- **Risks:**
 - Term-premium shock (long-end shoots wider) reverses the move.
 - Supply surprise at the August refunding cheapens 10s.
 - Risk-off flight-to-quality bull-flattens the curve.
- **Invalidates the thesis:** Spread breaches the stop in a single session on a known catalyst (NFP / FOMC / refunding).
